channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: 118th Congress was one of the least productive in over 30 years
item.description: The outgoing Congress enacted fewer than 210 bills into law over the last two years, with most enacted in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Elina Shirazi, NewsNation

item.title: The rise in mail voting comes with a price, as mismatched signatures lead to ballot rejections
item.description: The use of mail ballots exploded in 2020 as states looked for ways to accommodate voters during the COVID-19 pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Audrey Mcavoy And Ayanna Alexander, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump calls it the 'center of the universe.' Mar-a-Lago is a magnet for those seeking influence
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — The cars begin lining up early in the morning to be screened by Secret Service agents under white tents near the fence that surrounds President-elect Donald Trump’s vast south Florida estate.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and MICHELLE L. PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Takeaways from AP's reporting on Sarah McBride, the first openly trans person elected to Congress
item.description: DOVER, Del. (AP) — Sarah McBride made history in Delaware as the first openly transgender state senator in the United States. Now she's making history again, recently elected as the first openly trans member of Congress.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A guide to funeral services for former President Jimmy Carter
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Funeral services honoring former President Jimmy Carter, who died Sunday at age 100, will be held in Georgia and Washington, D.C., beginning Saturday, Jan. 4, and concluding Thursday, Jan. 9. Here's a list of events, as provided by the Joint Task Force-National Capital Region.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How Jimmy Carter paved way for craft beer
item.description: A bill signed by then-President Jimmy Carter helped spark America's craft beer industry. Panelists weigh in on his legacy.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation

item.title: ICE shuts down programs offering services to illegal immigrants, citing ‘immense’ costs
item.description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement says it has stopped two programs related to service for illegal immigrants as they did not align with the agency's mission.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Army National Guard soldier from Indiana dies in non-combat incident in Iraq
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A 34-year-old soldier serving with the Indiana National Guard in Iraq has died in a non-combat incident, the Army said Monday. Capt. Eric Richard Hart, 34, of Indianapolis, Indiana, died Saturday in Iraq, the Army National Guard said in a statement.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Treasury says Chinese hackers remotely accessed workstations, documents in 'major' cyber incident
item.description: The Treasury Department said Chinese hackers remotely accessed several employee workstations and unclassified documents after compromising a third-party software service provider.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Biden announces nearly $2.5 billion more in military aid for Ukraine
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Monday that the United States will send nearly $2.5 billion more in weapons to Ukraine as his administration works quickly to spend all the money it has available to help Kyiv fight off Russia before President-elect Donald Trump takes office.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US appeals court upholds Trump verdict in E. Jean Carroll defamation case
item.description: In a separate case, Trump was also ordered by a jury to pay Carroll more than $80 million in damages for the defamatory statements, though the president-elect’s appeal of that decision is still pending.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A caucus of military veterans seeks to bridge the political divide in a polarized Congress
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The players in this year's Army-Navy football game kept up a long tradition, with each side honoring the other’s school song when the contest ended. It was an acknowledgment that the future could bring moments when the opponents that day are teammates on deadlier fields.
item.dc:creator: GARY FIELDS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How American presidents have planned their own funerals
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jimmy Carter's memorial journey will end at his house in the tiny town of Plains, Georgia, where he grew up on a peanut farm. That is where his wife, Rosalynn, was laid to rest last year in a burial plot that they chose years ago.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter is an example of ‘simple decency': Joe Biden
item.description: President Biden said former President Jimmy Carter, who died Sunday, “lived a life measured not by words but by deeds.
item.dc:creator: Brian Howard, NewsNation

item.title: Geraldo Rivera recalls Jimmy Carter's life, legacy
item.description: "He was a wonderful president and even better in his post-presidential life," Geraldo Rivera said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: In Photos: Jimmy Carter
item.description: Former President Jimmy Carter died on Sunday, according to the Carter Center. Here's a look at his life and times in photos. He had turned 100 in October, making him the longest-lived president in the nation’s history.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, NewsNation

item.title: Musk causes uproar for backing Germany's far-right party ahead of key elections
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Tech entrepreneur Elon Musk caused uproar after backing Germany’s far-right party in a major newspaper ahead of key parliamentary elections in the Western European country, leading to the resignation of the paper’s opinion editor in protest.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: In shift, Trump downgrades soaring rhetoric on campaign promises
item.description: President-elect Trump on the campaign trail made grandiose promises to voters to bring down costs quickly, to end the war in Ukraine before he even took office and to use tariffs to bolster the U.S. economy and manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Another Florida state representative switches from Democrat to Republican
item.description: For the second time this year, a Florida Democratic lawmaker has switched their party affiliation after winning their seat.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Baja to lease warehouse space to house deported migrants
item.description: He also stated the warehouse location will have easier access from the ports of entry where migrants might be deported instead of having to bus them to several areas in the city.
item.dc:creator: Salvador Rivera, Fox 5
